name: Oakdale
description_short: Oakdale Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving the Oakdale, Wisconsin community with comprehensive banking services including checking, savings, loans, and digital banking tools.
description_long:
Oakdale Credit Union (Oakdale CU) is a community-based financial cooperative headquartered in Oakdale, Wisconsin, operating under NCUA oversight. The organization serves members through a full suite of traditional and digital banking services designed to support personal and business financial needs in the Oakdale area and surrounding service regions.

Oakdale CU offers a comprehensive range of deposit and lending products. On the savings side, they provide regular share savings accounts (13,969 active accounts), money market accounts ($3.5M in balances across 57 accounts), share certificates/CDs (529 accounts), and tax-advantaged retirement accounts including IRAs and KEOGH plans (378 accounts). Checking services include free share draft accounts (6,915 active accounts) with no monthly fees and free checks. Lending products mentioned include auto loans and mortgage options. Digital services include online and mobile banking, bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and online loan payments.

As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, Oakdale CU operates on a member-owned cooperative model, which typically results in lower fees and competitive rates compared to traditional banks. The institution emphasizes personalized service with dedicated financial planning and wealth management options, investment and retirement planning expertise, and exclusive member benefits. The credit union maintains physical branch access with a branch locator service and provides direct contact support via phone (608-372-3939).

Oakdale CU is well-established with a substantial membership base, though the website content is generic service descriptions rather than detailed product specifications, rates, or fee structures. The institution appears solid for members seeking traditional community banking with digital convenience, though specific rate competitiveness and fee schedules would require direct inquiry. The focus on member benefits and lower-cost structure are typical credit union advantages. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Share Savings Accounts (regular savings with competitive rates)
Money Market Accounts (higher-yield flexible savings/checking hybrid)
Share Certificates/CDs (certificates of deposit for long-term savings)
IRA and KEOGH retirement accounts (tax-advantaged retirement planning)
Share Draft Checking Accounts (free checking with debit cards and checks)
Auto Loans and Mortgage Loans (flexible lending options)
Online Banking and Mobile Banking (digital account management)
Bill Pay Services (online bill payment with scheduling)
Financial Planning and Wealth Management (personalized investment guidance)
pros: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees and free share drafts
Member-owned credit union model typically offering lower fees and competitive rates
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile banking, bill pay, and remote deposit capture
Tax-advantaged retirement account options (IRA/KEOGH) for long-term savings
Large active membership base with 13,969 savings accounts and 6,915 checking accounts
Full-service financial planning and wealth management services available
NCUA-insured deposits providing federal protection for member funds
cons: Limited geographic footprint serving primarily Oakdale, Wisconsin and surrounding areas
No specific interest rates, APYs, or loan terms disclosed on the services page
Website provides generic service descriptions without detailed fee schedules or product specifications
No information about minimum balance requirements or account limitations
Limited loan product details (auto and mortgage mentioned but no specifics on terms, rates, or eligibility)
best_for: Oakdale, Wisconsin residents seeking community-based banking with member ownership benefits
Savers prioritizing competitive rates and low-fee products through a credit union structure
Members planning retirement who want tax-advantaged accounts and financial planning expertise
Small business owners needing comprehensive personal and business financial services
diagnosis: Oakdale Credit Union is best suited for Oakdale-area residents and broader service region members who prioritize community banking, member benefits, and lower-fee products typical of credit union membership. The main caveat is that specific rates, fees, and product terms are not disclosed on their services page, requiring direct contact with the institution for detailed comparison shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
